title: CBI Officer
description: A CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation) Officer investigates crimes of national and international importance, including corruption, economic offenses, and special crimes. They gather evidence, interrogate suspects, and build strong cases for prosecution.
content: CBI Officers play a crucial role in upholding justice and maintaining law and order in India. The job requires strong analytical skills, integrity, and the ability to work under pressure. Officers often work long hours and travel extensively. Entry is typically through the Civil Services Examination (CSE) conducted by the UPSC or through promotion from within the police force.

financialFeasibility.note: While the UPSC exam is accessible to all, coaching and resources can be expensive. Financial stability helps in focusing on preparation without added stress.

sectorScore.remarks: CBI is a government organization. Private sector opportunities are virtually non-existent for this specific role.

relatedCareers.reason: Shares similar skills in investigation, law enforcement, and maintaining order.
relatedCareers.title: Police Officer

relatedCareers.reason: Requires strong analytical and legal knowledge, crucial for building strong cases.
relatedCareers.title: Lawyer

relatedCareers.reason: Involves gathering information, analyzing data, and working to protect national security.
relatedCareers.title: Intelligence Officer

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, there is increasing representation of women in the CBI and other law enforcement agencies.
